steps.title: Model the graphed line
steps.content:
The graph shown is $y=f(x)-11$. Two clear points on that line are $(-1,2)$ and $(0,-2)$. Their slope is
$$\frac{-2-2}{0-(-1)}=-4$$
Since the line crosses the $y$-axis at $-2$, the graphed line is $y=-4x-2$.

steps.title: Recover the original function
steps.content: The graphed line represents $f(x)-11$, so set the expression equal to the line and solve for $f(x)$: $$f(x)-11=-4x-2$$ $$f(x)=-4x+9$$ The answer is B.
